title: Mission & Witness
description: God's purpose to bless all nations through his people. From Abraham's call to be a blessing to Israel as a light to the nations to the Great Commission, mission is not an appendix to Scripture but its heartbeat.

stops.label: Blessed to Be a Blessing
stops.development: God calls Abraham and promises: 'In you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.' Election is for mission, not privilege.
stops.what_changes: The purpose of Israel's calling is defined from the outset. Blessing is centrifugal: received in order to be given.

stops.label: Kingdom of Priests
stops.development: Israel is called to be a 'kingdom of priests and a holy nation.' Priests mediate between God and others; Israel mediates for the world.
stops.what_changes: National identity is missional. Israel exists not for its own sake but as a priestly bridge between God and the nations.

stops.label: Light to the Nations
stops.development: God declares: 'I will make you as a light for the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth.'
stops.what_changes: Mission's scope becomes explicitly universal. The Servant's task is not merely to restore Israel but to bring salvation to the ends of the earth.

stops.label: Nineveh Repents
stops.development: Jonah reluctantly preaches; Nineveh repents in sackcloth and ashes. God relents from the disaster he had declared.
stops.what_changes: Mission to the nations is shown to be effective, even when the messenger is reluctant. Gentile repentance is genuine and accepted.

stops.label: The Great Commission
stops.development: 'Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them... teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you.'
stops.what_changes: Mission becomes the church's defining task. The Abrahamic promise of blessing to all families takes its final commissioning form.

stops.label: Witnesses to the Ends of the Earth
stops.development: Jesus maps the mission: 'You will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.'
stops.what_changes: Mission has a geographic and demographic trajectory: from center to periphery, from familiar to foreign, from Jew to Gentile.

stops.label: Paul Turns to the Gentiles
stops.development: Paul and Barnabas quote Isaiah 49:6: 'I have made you a light for the Gentiles.' The Servant's mission is taken up by the apostolic church.
stops.what_changes: Isaiah's vision is enacted. The prophetic promise of universal mission becomes apostolic practice.

stops.label: Every Nation, Tribe, Tongue
stops.development: A great multitude from every nation stands before the throne, crying 'Salvation belongs to our God!' The mission is accomplished.
stops.what_changes: The arc from Genesis 12:3 reaches its fulfillment. Every family of the earth is represented in the redeemed community.
